task_context.description: This is a multi-objective detector design optimization task that combines physics simulation with economic analysis. The user wants to systematically evaluate three different electromagnetic calorimeter configurations (premium PbWO4 crystals, mid-range lead-scintillator sampling, and budget iron-scintillator sampling) to find the optimal balance between detector performance and cost. The task involves running Geant4 simulations to measure energy resolution and other performance metrics, calculating material costs based on provided cost models, and performing a cost-benefit analysis to identify which design provides the best energy resolution per dollar spent. This is essentially a Pareto optimization problem in the performance-cost space for particle detector design.
task_context.goal: Find the electromagnetic calorimeter configuration that gives the best energy resolution per dollar
constraints: All designs should target ~20 radiation lengths total depth
Energy sweep limited to [1, 5, 20] GeV for tractable simulation
Must use electrons (e-) in Geant4 particle gun
Use specific material cost model provided

observations.content: This workflow systematically addresses the multi-objective optimization problem by first designing three distinct calorimeter configurations targeting the same physics depth but with different cost profiles. The approach maximizes computational efficiency by generating all geometries together and running a comprehensive simulation that tests all configurations across the required energy range in a single step. The analysis phase extracts the critical performance metrics (energy resolution parameterization, shower containment) and combines them with detailed cost calculations to perform the cost-benefit analysis. The workflow respects the hard constraint of maximum 2 simulation steps while thoroughly evaluating the Pareto frontier in the performance-cost space to identify the optimal electromagnetic calorimeter design.

Step completed successfully: Calculate geometry parameters for three calorimeter configurations targeting ~20 radiation lengths depth: Premium PbWO4 homogeneous, Mid-range Pb+scintillator sampling, Budget Fe+scintillator sampling
